parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Enable or disable the MIDI In .

parameters.name: source
parameters.label: MIDI Source
parameters.description: Get MIDI input from a device or a file.

parameters.name: device
parameters.label: Device Table
parameters.description: Path to the MIDI device Table DAT

parameters.name: id
parameters.label: Device ID
parameters.description: Specify the id of which device to use.

parameters.name: file
parameters.label: MIDI File
parameters.description: If MIDI file is selected as the MIDI source use this field to specify the name of the MIDI file to read. The file can be read in from disk or from the web. Use http:// when specifying a URL.

parameters.name: entire
parameters.label: Read Entire MIDI File
parameters.description: If enabled, the entire MIDI file is read. Otherwise, the Start and End parameters on the Channel page determine the segment of the file to read.

parameters.name: resetchannels
parameters.label: Reset	Channels
parameters.description: Deletes all channels when set to On, new channels will not be added until Reset Channels is turned Off.

parameters.name: resetchannelspulse
parameters.label: Reset Channels Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly deletes all channels.

parameters.name: simplified
parameters.label: Simplified Output
parameters.description: When this is on channels are automatically created when MIDI signal is detected from the selected MIDI device.

parameters.name: preservepulses
parameters.label: Preserve Pulses
parameters.description: When on, quick value transitions (pulses) are spaced out over consecutive output samples. Use this option when pulse frequencies approach or exceed the timeline rate, otherwise they risk overlapping e

parameters.name: onebased
parameters.label: 1 Based Index
parameters.description: Make the index 1 based instead of the default 0 based.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.label: MIDI Channels
parameters.description: The may read from any number of MIDI channels, numbered 1-16. Ranges and multiple entries are supported (i.e. "1 4 6", "1-7 12", "1-5:2").

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.label: Channel Prefix
parameters.description: When recording from multiple MIDI channels, putting a string like "ch" in this parameter causes the MIDI channel to be split into separate channels per MIDI channel. Otherwise the MIDI channels are al

parameters.name: recordtype
parameters.label: Record Method
parameters.description: Determine what to record.

parameters.name: record
parameters.label: Record
parameters.description: This parameter is used as a button to start and stop recording into the channels.

parameters.name: notename
parameters.label: Note Name
parameters.description: Put an "n" in here to generate channels for note events. It is the base name of the channel used to record notes. If blank, notes are ignored. If the Note Output parameter is set to Separate Channels,

parameters.name: notescope
parameters.label: Note Scope
parameters.description: The scope of notes to record. Multiple ranges and notes can be recorded (i.e., "50-60", "64 65 66 70-80").

parameters.name: notemeth
parameters.label: Note Output
parameters.description: Describes how multiple notes are handled. (As one channel, or individual).

parameters.name: velocity
parameters.label: Velocity
parameters.description: Describes how multiple velocity events are recorded. (Separate channels or combined in one channel as separate samples).

parameters.name: velname
parameters.label: Velocity Name
parameters.description: When Velocity is set to Separate Channels, this parameter is the base name of the Velocity channel (try "v"). If blank, no velocity channels will be recorded.

parameters.name: aftername
parameters.label: Aftertouch Name
parameters.description: The base name of the polyphonic Aftertouch channels. One aftertouch channel is created for each note in the Note . If blank, no aftertouch channels will be created.

parameters.name: pressname
parameters.label: Pressure Name
parameters.description: The name of the Channel Pressure channel. If multiple channels are being recorded, all channel pressure changes of interest will be recording on this channel. If blank, this channel will not be create

parameters.name: notenorm
parameters.label: Normalize
parameters.description: Note values can be normalized for convenience:

parameters.name: pitchname
parameters.label: Pitch Wheel Name
parameters.description: The name of the Pitch Wheel channel. Pitch wheel values range from -1 to +1. If blank, this channel will not be created. Put "p" in here to generate a channel.

parameters.name: controlname
parameters.label: Controller Name
parameters.description: The base name of the Control Change channels. The channel names are appended with the controller index (0-127). If blank, control changes will not be recorded. It typically contains "c".

parameters.name: controltype
parameters.label: Controller Type
parameters.description: There are 128 different controllers available. By choosing By Index Only, you can specify any number of controllers in the Control Index parameter. Otherwise, you can select a controller from the list

parameters.name: controlind
parameters.label: Controller Index
parameters.description: Used to select controllers by number, or multiple controllers by ranges. When in By Index Only Controller Type mode, you can select up to the full 128 controllers or sub-ranges thereof (i.e. "1-10", "

parameters.name: norm
parameters.label: Normalize
parameters.description: Controller values can be normalized for convenience:

parameters.name: unwrap
parameters.label: Unwrap
parameters.description: When on, values do not jump between min and max, but are modified to be continuous ramps. Use with knob controllers for examples.

parameters.name: progname
parameters.label: Program Change
parameters.description: The name of the Program Change channel. All program change messages will be recorded onto this channel. If blank, this channel will not be created.

parameters.name: pulsename
parameters.label: Timer Pulse Name
parameters.description: Record timer pulse messages.

parameters.name: rampname
parameters.label: Timer Ramp Name
parameters.description: Record timer ramp messages.

parameters.name: timerperiod
parameters.label: Timer Period
parameters.description: Record timer period messages.

parameters.name: timerstart
parameters.label: Timer Start
parameters.description: Record timer start messages.

parameters.name: ticks
parameters.label: Ticks per Beat
parameters.description: Specify the expected ticks per beat. This will influence the timer and bar output values.

parameters.name: barname
parameters.label: Bar Ramp Name
parameters.description: Output the current bar position.

parameters.name: barperiod
parameters.label: Bar Period
parameters.description: Output the current bar period events.

parameters.name: barstart
parameters.label: Bar Start
parameters.description: Output when a bar starts.

parameters.name: barmsg
parameters.label: Bar Message
parameters.description: Capture bar messages. Place a V in the message to specify which value the channel should have.

parameters.name: songpos
parameters.label: Song Pos Name
parameters.description: Capture song position messages.

parameters.name: sysex
parameters.label: System Exclusive
parameters.description: Sequence of system exclusive message handlers

parameters.name: sysex0name
parameters.label: Channel Name
parameters.description: Name of a specific exclusive message to be captured.

parameters.name: sysex0msg
parameters.label: Message
parameters.description: Contents of the exclusive message. Place a V in the message to specify which value the channel should have.

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Defines where recording begins. In "Tie to Time Line" mode, any events received before the start time will be ignored. In "Time Line Independent" mode, recording will start at this point and continue

parameters.name: startunit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: Defines the end of the segment to read for MIDI Files.

parameters.name: endunit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: Defines the sample rate of this , in samples per second. If the sample rate is too low, a rapidly changing input may be misrepresented.

parameters.name: left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: The left extend conditions (before range).

parameters.name: right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: The right extend conditions (after range).

parameters.name: defval
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: sortchannels
parameters.label: Sortchannels
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Resetpulse
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sortchannelspulse
parameters.label: Sortchannelspulse
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: legacypattern
parameters.label: Legacypattern
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

examples.label: Configure MIDI Channels and Legacypattern
examples.code:
n = op('midiin1')
n.par.channel = '1-16'
n.par.legacypattern = True
n.par.timeslice = True
examples.description: Common settings from 4 real Derivative examples
